sections.paragraph:
καρδιογνώστης, ου, ὁ (s. καρδία, γνώστης; only in Christian wr., e.g. Ps.-Clem., Hom. 10, 13; other reff. in Haenchen ad loc.) knower of hearts, one who knows the hearts, of God Ac 1:24; 15:8 (on these pass. s. JBauer, BZ 32, ’88, 114–17); Hm 4, 3, 4.—M-M. DELG s.v. γιγνώσκω. TW.

  1. wr. = writers, writings

e.g. e.g. = exempli gratia (for example)

Ps. Ps. = pseudo, pseudonymous

Hom Hom , VIII b.c.—List 5

Haenchen Haenchen = EH., Commentary on Acts—List 6

ad loc. ad loc. = ad locum (to or at the place under consideration)

pass. pass. = passive (either of grammatical form or of passive experience); also used in reference to literary portion=passage

BZ BZ = Biblische Zeitschrift—List 6

Hm Hm = Mandates

M-M M-M = JMoulton/GMilligan, Vocabulary of Greek Testament—Lists 4, 6

DELG DELG = PChantraine, Dictionnaire étymologique de la langue grecque—List 6

s.v. s.v. = sub voce (under the word, look up the word)

TW TW = Theologisches Wörterbuch zum NT; tr. GBromiley, Theological Dictionary of the NT—List 6

Arndt, William ; Danker, Frederick W. ; Bauer, Walter: A Greek-English Lexicon of the New Testament and Other Early Christian Literature. 3rd ed. Chicago : University of Chicago Press, 2000, S. 509
